    11	export function computeBlockFingerprint(text: string): string {
    12	  const input = text.slice(0, 200) + String(text.length)
    13	  let hash = 5381
    14	  for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {
    15	    hash = ((hash << 5) + hash + (input.codePointAt(i) ?? 0)) | 0
    16	  }
    17	  return (hash >>> 0).toString(16)
    18	}
    19	
    20	function getNodeType(node: Record<string, any>): string {
    21	  const t = node.type as string

     9	type CommentWithAnchor = CommentModel & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
    10	
    11	function collectRangeAnchors(comments: CommentWithAnchor[]): RangeAnchor[] {
    12	  const result: RangeAnchor[] = []
    13	  for (const c of comments) {
    14	    if (c.anchor?.mode === 'range') result.push(c.anchor)
    15	    if (c.children) {
    16	      result.push(...collectRangeAnchors(c.children as CommentWithAnchor[]))
    17	    }
    18	  }
    19	  return result
    20	}
    21	
    22	function findBlockElement(
    23	  contentEl: Element,
    24	  blockInfos: BlockInfo[],
    25	  blockId: string,
    26	): Element | null {
    27	  const idx = blockInfos.findIndex((b) => b.blockId === blockId)
    28	  if (idx === -1) return null
    29	  return contentEl.children[idx] ?? null
    30	}
    31	
    32	function createDomRange(
    33	  blockEl: Element,
    34	  startOffset: number,
    35	  endOffset: number,
    36	): Range | null {
    37	  const walker = document.createTreeWalker(blockEl, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    38	  let offset = 0
    39	  let startNode: Text | null = null
    40	  let startNodeOffset = 0
    41	  let endNode: Text | null = null
    42	  let endNodeOffset = 0
    43	  let node: Node | null
    44	
    45	  while ((node = walker.nextNode())) {
    46	    const textNode = node as Text
    47	    const len = textNode.length
    48	
    49	    if (!startNode && offset + len >= startOffset) {
    50	      startNode = textNode
    51	      startNodeOffset = startOffset - offset
    52	    }
    53	    if (offset + len >= endOffset) {
    54	      endNode = textNode
    55	      endNodeOffset = endOffset - offset
    56	      break
    57	    }
    58	    offset += len
    59	  }
    60	
    61	  if (!startNode || !endNode) return null
    62	
    63	  const range = document.createRange()
    64	  range.setStart(startNode, startNodeOffset)
    65	  range.setEnd(endNode, endNodeOffset)
    66	  return range
    67	}
    68	
    69	export function CommentAnchorHighlight({
    70	  containerRef,
    71	  blockInfos,
    72	  comments,
    73	}: {
    74	  containerRef: React.RefObject<HTMLElement | null>

    76	  comments: CommentWithAnchor[]

    78	  useEffect(() => {

    82	    const contentEl = containerRef.current.querySelector('.rich-content')
    83	    if (!contentEl) return
    84	
    85	    const rangeAnchors = collectRangeAnchors(comments)
    86	    if (rangeAnchors.length === 0) {

    89	    }
    90

    94	      if (!blockEl) continue

    99

    15	export const commentStoragePrefix = 'comment-'
    16	export const createInitialValue = () => ({
    17	  refId: atom(''),
    18	
    19	  text: atom(''),
    20	  author: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}author`, ''),
    21	  mail: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}mail`, ''),
    22	  url: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}url`, ''),
    23	
    24	  avatar: atom(''),
    25	  source: atom(''),
    26

    29	  // settings
    30	  isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}is-whisper`, false),
    31	  syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(

    33	    true,

    35	})
    36	export const CommentBoxContext = createContext<
    37	  ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    38	>(null!)
    39	
    40	export const CommentBoxLifeCycleContext = createContext<{
    41	  afterSubmit?: () => void
    42	}>(null!)
    43	
    44	export const CommentBoxProvider = (
    45	  props: PropsWithChildren & {
    46	    refId: string
    47	    afterSubmit?: () => void
    48	    initialValue?: string
    49	    anchor?: CommentAnchor | null
    50	  },
    51	) => {
    52	  const { refId, children, afterSubmit, initialValue, anchor } = props
    53	
    54	  const ctxValue = useMemo(
    55	    () => ({
    56	      ...createInitialValue(),
    57	      refId: atom(refId),
    58	    }),
    59	    [refId],
    60	  )
    61	  useBeforeMounted(() => {
    62	    if (initialValue) {
    63	      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.text, initialValue)
    64	    }
    65	    if (anchor) {
    66	      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.anchor, anchor)
    67	    }
    68	  })
    69	  return (
    70	    <CommentBoxContext key={refId} value={ctxValue}>
    71	      <CommentBoxLifeCycleContext
    72	        value={useMemo(() => ({ afterSubmit }), [afterSubmit])}
    73	      >
    74	        {children}
    75	      </CommentBoxLifeCycleContext>
    76	    </CommentBoxContext>
    77	  )
    78	}
    79	
    80	export const CommentIsReplyContext = createContext(false)
    81	export const CommentOriginalRefIdContext = createContext('')
    82	export const CommentCompletedCallbackContext = createContext<
    83	  null | ((comment: CommentModel) => void)
    84	>(null)
    85	export const CommentIsReplyProvider = (
    86	  props: PropsWithChildren<{
    87	    isReply: boolean
    88	    originalRefId: string
    89	    onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
    90	  }>,
    91	) => {
    92	  const { isReply, originalRefId, onCompleted, children } = props
    93	  return (
    94	    <CommentOriginalRefIdContext value={originalRefId}>
    95	      <CommentIsReplyContext value={isReply}>
    96	        <CommentCompletedCallbackContext value={onCompleted || null}>
    97	          {children}
    98	        </CommentCompletedCallbackContext>
    99	      </CommentIsReplyContext>
   100	    </CommentOriginalRefIdContext>
   101	  )
   102	}
   103	
   104	export const CommentBoxSlotPortal = memo((props: PropsWithChildren) => {
   105	  const { children } = props
   106	  useEffect(() => {
   107	    setCommentActionLeftSlot(children)
   108	    return () => {
   109	      setCommentActionLeftSlot(null)
   110	    }
   111	  }, [children])
   112	  return null
   113	})
   114	
   115	export const CommentBoxSlotProvider: FC = memo(() => useCommentActionLeftSlot())

   117	CommentBoxSlotProvider.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotProvider'
   118	CommentBoxSlotPortal.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotPortal'

   183	export function RichRenderer({
   184	  value,
   185	  variant = 'article',
   186	  theme = 'light',
   187	  className,
   188	  style,
   189	  as: Component = 'div',
   190	  rendererConfig,
   191	  extraNodes,
   192	}: RichRendererProps) {

   194	  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
   195	
   196	  const { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent } = renderEditorToReact(
   197	    value,
   198	    nodes,
   199	  )
   200	
   201	  const classes = ['rich-content', variantClass, className]
   202	    .filter(Boolean)
   203	    .join(' ')
   204	
   205	  return (
   206	    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass} theme={theme}>
   207	      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
   208	        <RendererConfigProvider
   209	          config={rendererConfig}
   210	          mode="renderer"
   211	          variant={variant}
   212	        >
   213	          <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
   214	            definitions={footnoteData.definitions}
   215	            displayNumberMap={footnoteData.displayNumberMap}
   216	          >
   217	            <NestedContentRendererProvider value={renderNestedContent}>
   218	              <Component className={classes} style={style} data-theme={theme}>
   219	                {content}
   220	              </Component>
   221	            </NestedContentRendererProvider>
   222	          </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
   223	        </RendererConfigProvider>
   224	      </ColorSchemeProvider>
   225	    </PortalThemeProvider>
   226	  )

    17	function findDomTextNode(
    18	  container: Element,
    19	  targetOffset: number,
    20	): { node: Text; offset: number } | null {
    21	  const walker = document.createTreeWalker(container, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    22	  let consumed = 0
    23	  let textNode: Text | null
    24	  while ((textNode = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    25	    const len = textNode.textContent?.length ?? 0
    26	    if (consumed + len >= targetOffset) {
    27	      return { node: textNode, offset: targetOffset - consumed }
    28	    }
    29	    consumed += len
    30	  }
    31	  return null
    32	}
    33	
    34	export function CommentHighlighter({
    35	  containerRef,
    36	  blockInfos,
    37	  comments,
    38	  activeId,
    39	}: CommentHighlighterProps) {
    40	  useEffect(() => {
    41	    if (typeof CSS === 'undefined' || !('highlights' in CSS)) return
    42	
    43	    const { highlights } = CSS as any as { highlights: Map<string, Highlight> }
    44	
    45	    function updateHighlights() {
    46	      const normalRanges: Range[] = []
    47	      const activeRanges: Range[] = []
    48	
    49	      const rangeComments = comments.filter((c) => c.anchor.mode === 'range')
    50	      if (rangeComments.length === 0) {
    51	        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
    52	        highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
    53	        return
    54	      }
    55	
    56	      const container = containerRef.current
    57	      if (!container) return
    58	      const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
    59	      if (!contentEl) return
    60	
    61	      for (const comment of rangeComments) {
    62	        const { anchor } = comment
    63	        if (anchor.mode !== 'range') continue
    64	
    65	        const blockIdx = blockInfos.findIndex(
    66	          (b) => b.blockId === anchor.blockId,
    67	        )
    68	        if (blockIdx === -1 || blockIdx >= contentEl.children.length) continue
    69	
    70	        const blockEl = contentEl.children[blockIdx]
    71	        const start = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.startOffset)
    72	        const end = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.endOffset)
    73	        if (!start || !end) continue
    74	
    75	        try {
    76	          const range = new Range()
    77	          range.setStart(start.node, start.offset)
    78	          range.setEnd(end.node, end.offset)
    79	          if (comment.id === activeId) {
    80	            activeRanges.push(range)
    81	          } else {
    82	            normalRanges.push(range)
    83	          }
    84	        } catch {
    85	          // offset out of bounds after layout change
    86	        }
    87	      }
    88	
    89	      if (normalRanges.length > 0) {
    90	        highlights.set('comment-highlight', new Highlight(...normalRanges))
    91	      } else {
    92	        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
    93	      }
    94	
    95	      if (activeRanges.length > 0) {
    96	        highlights.set(
    97	          'comment-highlight-active',
    98	          new Highlight(...activeRanges),
    99	        )

   102	      }
   103	    }

    28	    }
    29	  }
    30	  return null
    31	}

</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-27 15:12:41Z)**_

I’ve completed the diff inspection and identified several concrete issues (including one high-confidence flow break and one highlight reactivity bug). I’m now assembling them in severity order with exact file/line references plus assumptions/testing gaps.

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-27 15:13:03Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Summarizing key code review findings**
</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-27 15:13:18Z)**_

**Findings**
1. **High**: Range anchor is captured too late and can be lost before submit.  
[WithArticleSelectionAction.tsx:83](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/shared/WithArticleSelectionAction.tsx:83), [WithArticleSelectionAction.tsx:156](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/shared/WithArticleSelectionAction.tsx:156)  
`buildAnchorFromCurrentSelection()` runs inside button `onClick`, after the user clicks the popup button. In browsers, that click can collapse/clear selection first, so anchor becomes `null` and the flow silently falls back to quote text.

2. **High**: Highlight rendering is not reactive to comment query updates.  
[LexicalCommentWrapper.tsx:35](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/LexicalCommentWrapper.tsx:35), [LexicalCommentWrapper.tsx:40](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/LexicalCommentWrapper.tsx:40)  
`queryClient.getQueryData(...)` is a snapshot read, not a subscription. When comments load/refresh, this component won’t re-render from query updates, so `CommentAnchorHighlight` can stay stale or empty until an unrelated re-render.

3. **Medium-High**: Offset math is inconsistent around line breaks, causing incorrect anchors/quotes in some blocks.  
[anchor-utils.ts:31](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/anchor-utils.ts:31), [anchor-utils.ts:89](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/anchor-utils.ts:89), [anchor-utils.ts:127](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/anchor-utils.ts:127)  
`getTextContent` counts `linebreak` as `\n`, but `getTextOffset` only walks text nodes (ignores `<br>`). For selections after line breaks, stored offsets and `quote/prefix/suffix` can drift.

4. **Medium**: Custom Highlight API safety/fallback handling is incomplete.  
[CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:79](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:79), [CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:101](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:101), [layer.css:497](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/styles/layer.css:497)  
Only `CSS.highlights` is checked; `window.Highlight` construction isn’t guarded, and range construction isn’t wrapped for bad anchor payloads. Unsupported browsers get no visual fallback at all (anchor info only appears in comment text).

5. **Medium**: `blockType` for heading anchors diverges from canonical rich-editor anchor shape.  
[anchor-utils.ts:20](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/anchor-utils.ts:20), [comment-anchor.ts:233](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/utils/comment-anchor.ts:233)  
Web extraction emits `heading-h1`/`heading-h2`, while canonical builder uses `heading`. If backend/consumers expect canonical values, heading anchors may not match or validate consistently.

6. **Low**: Type safety is bypassed at key anchor boundaries.  
[hooks.tsx:147](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/hooks.tsx:147), [Comment.tsx:108](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/Comment.tsx:108), [CommentModal.tsx:35](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/shared/CommentModal.tsx:35)  
Outbound payload uses `anchor?: any`, and inbound comment anchors are cast. This removes compile-time guarantees and leaves potential runtime errors if stored anchor shape is malformed.

**Open Questions / Assumptions**
1. I assumed anchor integrity is not fully guaranteed by backend validation; if backend strictly validates anchor schema, some runtime-risk items are lower.
2. I assumed inline highlight is expected to work after async comment loading, not only after full page re-render.
3. I did not run tests/typecheck in this review pass.
